title: artemisia marschalliana subsp. tschernieviana
intro: Artemisia marschalliana subsp. tschernieviana, also known as Russian wormwood, is a subspecies of the Artemisia marschalliana plant, a member of the daisy family native to Central Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Russian wormwood include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an antioxidant, protecting against cell damage and promoting overall well-being. Russian wormwood is often prepared as an infusion, tincture, or essential oil, and can be used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
